Notice_Of_Motion_To_File_Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alex Cheung, Esq. 415-986-0688 of Lorber Greenfield Polito for Defendant.
Thomas Gaffney, Esq. 310-773-3280 of Olsen & Brueggemann, APC for Defendant TKE through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PEMBROKE REAL ESTATE, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Pembroke Real Estate, Inc.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Corporation's opposition is groundless. A motion for leave to file an amended pleading need not be supported by admissible evidence supporting the factual accuracy of the new claims. If Thyssenkrupp wishes to challenge the proposed claims, it may do so by demurring or moving for summary adjudication to the proposed cross-complaint. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].) Thyssenkrupp has not shown that Pembroke has unduly delayed or that it will be prejudiced, given the April 27, 2020 trial date.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
